title: Psalm 1 — Two Ways Chiasm
description: Psalm 1 exhibits an A-B-C-B'-A' chiastic structure contrasting the righteous and the wicked.

elements.label: A
elements.summary: Blessed is the man who does NOT walk/stand/sit with wicked

elements.label: B
elements.summary: Delight in the law of the LORD — meditation

elements.label: C
elements.summary: CENTER: Tree planted by streams — prosperity

elements.label: B'
elements.summary: The wicked are NOT so — chaff

elements.label: A'
elements.summary: Wicked will NOT stand in judgment; LORD knows the way of the righteous

title: The Flood Narrative — Extended Chiasm
description: The flood narrative in Genesis 6-9 has a well-recognized chiastic structure centering on 'God remembered Noah' (8:1).

elements.label: B
elements.summary: Violence on earth; God commands ark (6:11-22)

elements.label: E'
elements.summary: CENTER: God remembered Noah; waters recede 150 days (8:1-5)

title: Romans 8:28-30 — The Golden Chain of Salvation
description: Paul presents a chain of five linked divine acts, each flowing into the next in a sorites (chain argument).

elements.label: Step 2
elements.summary: Predestined → Conformed to image of Son

title: Amos 1-2 — Oracle Pattern (3+1 Formula)
description: Eight oracles against nations follow the same pattern: 'For three transgressions of X, and for four...' spiraling from distant nations inward to Judah and finally Israel.

title: Sermon on the Mount — Three Pillars Structure
description: The Sermon on the Mount follows a teaching structure: Beatitudes (identity), Antitheses (ethics), and Three Practices (piety), bookended by kingdom declarations.

elements.label: Antitheses
elements.summary: 'You have heard... but I say' (6 contrasts)

elements.label: Warnings
elements.summary: Judging, pearls, narrow gate, false prophets

elements.label: Two Ways
elements.summary: Wise and foolish builders — closing parable

title: John 1:1-18 — Prologue Chiasm
description: The Johannine Prologue forms a chiasm centering on the incarnation ('the Word became flesh').

elements.label: A
elements.summary: The Word was with God and was God

elements.label: E
elements.summary: The true light; world did not know him

elements.label: F
elements.summary: Those who received him became children of God

elements.label: D'
elements.summary: Law through Moses; grace through Jesus Christ

elements.label: A'
elements.summary: No one has seen God; the Son has made him known

title: Psalm 119 — Grand Acrostic Structure
description: Psalm 119 is the longest chapter in the Bible: 22 stanzas of 8 verses each, one for each letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Each verse in a stanza begins with the same Hebrew letter.

elements.label: Aleph
elements.summary: Blessedness of walking in God's law

elements.label: Lamed
elements.summary: Your word is eternal in heaven

elements.label: Mem
elements.summary: Oh how I love your law!

elements.label: Nun
elements.summary: Your word is a lamp to my feet

elements.label: Samekh
elements.summary: Uphold me according to your promise

elements.label: Ayin
elements.summary: Time for the LORD to act

elements.label: Qoph
elements.summary: I cry with my whole heart

elements.label: Resh
elements.summary: Plead my cause and redeem me

elements.label: Tav
elements.summary: Let my cry come before you; I have gone astray like a lost sheep

title: Seven Letters of Revelation — Mirror Pattern
description: The seven letters to churches may form a chiastic pattern: Ephesus/Laodicea (lukewarm), Smyrna/Philadelphia (faithful), Pergamum/Sardis (compromise), with Thyatira at the center as longest letter.

elements.label: A
elements.summary: Ephesus — lost first love; threat of lampstand removal

elements.label: B
elements.summary: Smyrna — faithful in persecution; no rebuke

elements.label: CENTER
elements.summary: Thyatira — longest letter; Jezebel; morning star promise

elements.label: B'
elements.summary: Philadelphia — faithful; open door; no rebuke

elements.label: A'
elements.summary: Laodicea — lukewarm; threat of being spit out

title: Isaiah 5 — Parable of the Vineyard with Woe Oracles
description: Isaiah 5 begins with a love song about a vineyard (vv.1-7) and ends with judgment (vv.25-30), forming an inclusio around six woe oracles.

elements.label: Opening
elements.summary: Vineyard song: expected grapes, got wild grapes

elements.label: Woe 3
elements.summary: Woe to those who drag sin with cords

elements.label: Woe 4
elements.summary: Woe to those who call evil good

elements.label: Woe 5
elements.summary: Woe to those wise in their own eyes

elements.label: Woe 6
elements.summary: Woe to heroes at drinking wine

title: Psalm 150 — Universal Praise Crescendo
description: Psalm 150 builds a crescendo of praise with three parallel sections: where to praise (vv.1-2), how to praise (vv.3-5), and who praises (v.6), all framed by Hallelujah inclusio.

elements.label: Where
elements.summary: In his sanctuary, in his mighty heavens

elements.label: Why
elements.summary: For his mighty deeds, his surpassing greatness

elements.label: How
elements.summary: Trumpet, lute, harp, tambourine, dance, strings, pipe, cymbals

elements.label: Who
elements.summary: Everything that has breath — universal scope
